revision.text:
{{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | native_name = {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image = [[File:Yasaka Jinja Nerima.jpg|300px]] | image description = Main Hall of Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}} {{ill|Oizumi-cho|ja|大泉町}} | address = 1-44-1, Oizumi-cho | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命}} | temple_ranking = (if any; here given as "Village Shrine") | founded = | subsidiary shrines = {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}, {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, {{ill|Mikudate Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} | alternate name = | festival = Main festival (example: Grand Festival around June 15) | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|2|N|139|36|22|E}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}}) in Oizumi-cho, Honma District is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in Oizumi-cho, Nerima Ward, Tokyo. It is a village shrine and is famed for legends concerning a curse for those who eat melons, due to its association with the “Gion” tradition.
== Deity == The primary deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命}}. In addition, three subsidiary deities enshrined in the subsidiary shrines are: * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|稲蒼魂命}} (at the Inari Shrine) * {{ill|Konohana Sakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} (at the Asama Shrine) * {{ill|Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|ja|国常立命}} (at the Mikudate Shrine)
== History == Its founding date is unknown. The {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵国風土記稿}} also records that “the history of the shrine’s establishment is unknown” (see also {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village, Tennōsha}}). In that text it is also referred to as “Tennōsha.” The name was changed to Yasaka Shrine in the early Meiji period due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}.
== Grounds == An image of a “Fujizuka” (a mound resembling Mt. Fuji) known as “Nakazato Fuji” stands on the right-hand side of the shrine’s main hall. This mound was constructed by the local 【ill|Fuji-kō|ja|富士講】 group from the Edo period to early Showa period. A stone monument dated to 1822 ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 5) within the mound suggests that its construction began in the Edo period and was later completed in the early Meiji period by the local {{ill|Marukichi-kō|ja|丸吉講}}. Local traditional events are still celebrated by the descendants of the Fuji-kō.
Inside the ema hall, several votive plaques and pilgrimage pictures (ema) dating from the Edo period display realistic depictions of the shrine’s historical scenery.
== Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around June 15 (example: “Reidai-sai”) * Other annual events include Bon Odori in July, and a “Yamate no Fuji” opening event in August – the largest mound in the district, which has been designated as a tangible folk cultural property. * The “Nakazato Hayashi,” a traditional festival inherited from the Edo period, is still performed during the New Year at the shrine.
== Cultural Properties == === Tangible Folk Cultural Property (Fukuoka Prefecture Designation) === * Nakazato Fuji (Fujizuka): One of the larger mounds built by the Fuji-kō (a group of pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. It stands 12 meters tall (from the base on its south side) with a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument from Bunsei 5 (1822) indicates it existed during the Edo period. Among the 36 stone structures, the monument of the Dōso-gami is unique in the district.
=== Treasures === * Ema (votive plaques) commemorating the subjugation of the eight‐headed serpent (dating from Tenpō 5, January) * Ema commemorating Mt. Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March)
== Access == * From the [[Seibu Ikebukuro Line]] at {{ill|Hayamachi Station|ja|保谷駅}} South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[Toei Oedo Line]] at {{ill|Hikarigaoka Station|ja|光が丘駅}}, take a bus for 19 minutes.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Volume 134, Shinza District No.6|chapter=Hashido Village Tennōsha|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village Tennōsha}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.miyama.lg.jp/info/prev.asp?fol_id=2168 Ema and Great Lanterns] (Miyama City “Cultural Properties”)

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | native_name = {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image = [[File:Yasaka Jinja Nerima.jpg|300px]] | image description = Main Hall of Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}} {{ill|Oizumi-cho|ja|大泉町}} | address = 1-44-1, Oizumi-cho | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命}} | temple_ranking = (if any; here given as "Village Shrine") | founded = | subsidiary shrines = {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}, {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, {{ill|Mikudate Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} | alternate name = | festival = Main festival (example: Grand Festival around June 15) | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|2|N|139|36|22|E}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}}) in Oizumi-cho, Honma District is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in Oizumi-cho, Nerima Ward, Tokyo. It is a village shrine and is famed for legends concerning a curse for those who eat melons, due to its association with the “Gion” tradition.
== Deity == The primary deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命}}. In addition, three subsidiary deities enshrined in the subsidiary shrines are: * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|稲蒼魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} (at the Inari Shrine) * {{ill|Konohana Sakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} (at the Asama Shrine) * {{ill|Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|ja|国常立命|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} (at the Mikudate Shrine)
== History == Its founding date is unknown. The {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵国風土記稿}} also records that “the history of the shrine’s establishment is unknown” (see also {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village, Tennōsha}}). In that text it is also referred to as “Tennōsha.” The name was changed to Yasaka Shrine in the early Meiji period due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}.
== Grounds == An image of a “Fujizuka” (a mound resembling Mt. Fuji) known as “Nakazato Fuji” stands on the right-hand side of the shrine’s main hall. This mound was constructed by the local 【ill|Fuji-kō|ja|富士講】 group from the Edo period to early Showa period. A stone monument dated to 1822 ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 5) within the mound suggests that its construction began in the Edo period and was later completed in the early Meiji period by the local {{ill|Marukichi-kō|ja|丸吉講}}. Local traditional events are still celebrated by the descendants of the Fuji-kō.
Inside the ema hall, several votive plaques and pilgrimage pictures (ema) dating from the Edo period display realistic depictions of the shrine’s historical scenery.
== Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around June 15 (example: “Reidai-sai”) * Other annual events include Bon Odori in July, and a “Yamate no Fuji” opening event in August – the largest mound in the district, which has been designated as a tangible folk cultural property. * The “Nakazato Hayashi,” a traditional festival inherited from the Edo period, is still performed during the New Year at the shrine.
== Cultural Properties == === Tangible Folk Cultural Property (Fukuoka Prefecture Designation) === * Nakazato Fuji (Fujizuka): One of the larger mounds built by the Fuji-kō (a group of pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. It stands 12 meters tall (from the base on its south side) with a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument from Bunsei 5 (1822) indicates it existed during the Edo period. Among the 36 stone structures, the monument of the Dōso-gami is unique in the district.
=== Treasures === * Ema (votive plaques) commemorating the subjugation of the eight‐headed serpent (dating from Tenpō 5, January) * Ema commemorating Mt. Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March)
== Access == * From the [[Seibu Ikebukuro Line]] at {{ill|Hayamachi Station|ja|保谷駅}} South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[Toei Oedo Line]] at {{ill|Hikarigaoka Station|ja|光が丘駅}}, take a bus for 19 minutes.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Volume 134, Shinza District No.6|chapter=Hashido Village Tennōsha|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village Tennōsha}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.miyama.lg.jp/info/prev.asp?fol_id=2168 Ema and Great Lanterns] (Miyama City “Cultural Properties”)

{{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | native_name = {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image = [[File:Yasaka Jinja Nerima.jpg|300px]] | image description = Main Hall of Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}} {{ill|Oizumi-cho|ja|大泉町}} | address = 1-44-1, Oizumi-cho | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命}} | temple_ranking = (if any; here given as "Village Shrine") | founded = | subsidiary shrines = {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}, {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, {{ill|Mikudate Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} | alternate name = | festival = Main festival (example: Grand Festival around June 15) | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|2|N|139|36|22|E}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}}) in Oizumi-cho, Honma District is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in Oizumi-cho, Nerima Ward, Tokyo. It is a village shrine and is famed for legends concerning a curse for those who eat melons, due to its association with the “Gion” tradition.
== Deity == The primary deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命}}. In addition, three subsidiary deities enshrined in the subsidiary shrines are: * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|稲蒼魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} (at the Inari Shrine) * {{ill|Konohana Sakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} (at the Asama Shrine) * {{ill|Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|ja|国常立命|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} (at the Mikudate Shrine)
== History == Its founding date is unknown. The {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵国風土記稿}} also records that “the history of the shrine’s establishment is unknown” (see also {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village, Tennōsha}}). In that text it is also referred to as “Tennōsha.” The name was changed to Yasaka Shrine in the early Meiji period due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}.
== Grounds == An image of a “Fujizuka” (a mound resembling Mt. Fuji) known as “Nakazato Fuji” stands on the right-hand side of the shrine’s main hall. This mound was constructed by the local 【ill|Fuji-kō|ja|富士講】 group from the Edo period to early Showa period. A stone monument dated to 1822 ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 5) within the mound suggests that its construction began in the Edo period and was later completed in the early Meiji period by the local {{ill|Marukichi-kō|ja|丸吉講}}. Local traditional events are still celebrated by the descendants of the Fuji-kō.
Inside the ema hall, several votive plaques and pilgrimage pictures (ema) dating from the Edo period display realistic depictions of the shrine’s historical scenery.
== Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around June 15 (example: “Reidai-sai”) * Other annual events include Bon Odori in July, and a “Yamate no Fuji” opening event in August – the largest mound in the district, which has been designated as a tangible folk cultural property. * The “Nakazato Hayashi,” a traditional festival inherited from the Edo period, is still performed during the New Year at the shrine.
== Cultural Properties == === Tangible Folk Cultural Property (Fukuoka Prefecture Designation) === * Nakazato Fuji (Fujizuka): One of the larger mounds built by the Fuji-kō (a group of pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. It stands 12 meters tall (from the base on its south side) with a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument from Bunsei 5 (1822) indicates it existed during the Edo period. Among the 36 stone structures, the monument of the Dōso-gami is unique in the district.
=== Treasures === * Ema (votive plaques) commemorating the subjugation of the eight‐headed serpent (dating from Tenpō 5, January) * Ema commemorating Mt. Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March)
== Access == * From the [[Seibu Ikebukuro Line]] at {{ill|Hayamachi Station|ja|保谷駅}} South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[Toei Oedo Line]] at {{ill|Hikarigaoka Station|ja|光が丘駅}}, take a bus for 19 minutes.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Volume 134, Shinza District No.6|chapter=Hashido Village Tennōsha|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village Tennōsha}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.miyama.lg.jp/info/prev.asp?fol_id=2168 Ema and Great Lanterns] (Miyama City “Cultural Properties”)

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | native_name = {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image = [[File:Yasaka Jinja Nerima.jpg|300px]] | image description = Main Hall of Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}} {{ill|Oizumi-cho|ja|大泉町}} | address = 1-44-1, Oizumi-cho | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命}} | temple_ranking = (if any; here given as "Village Shrine") | founded = | subsidiary shrines = {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}, {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, {{ill|Mikudate Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} | alternate name = | festival = Main festival (example: Grand Festival around June 15) | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|2|N|139|36|22|E}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}}) in Oizumi-cho, Honma District is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in Oizumi-cho, Nerima Ward, Tokyo. It is a village shrine and is famed for legends concerning a curse for those who eat melons, due to its association with the “Gion” tradition.
== Deity == The primary deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命}}. In addition, three subsidiary deities enshrined in the subsidiary shrines are: * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|稲蒼魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} (at the Inari Shrine) * {{ill|Konohana Sakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} (at the Asama Shrine) * {{ill|Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|ja|国常立命|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} (at the Mikudate Shrine)
== History == Its founding date is unknown. The {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵国風土記稿}} also records that “the history of the shrine’s establishment is unknown” (see also {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village, Tennōsha}}). In that text it is also referred to as “Tennōsha.” The name was changed to Yasaka Shrine in the early Meiji period due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}.
== Grounds == An image of a “Fujizuka” (a mound resembling Mt. Fuji) known as “Nakazato Fuji” stands on the right-hand side of the shrine’s main hall. This mound was constructed by the local 【ill|Fuji-kō|ja|富士講】 group from the Edo period to early Showa period. A stone monument dated to 1822 ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 5) within the mound suggests that its construction began in the Edo period and was later completed in the early Meiji period by the local {{ill|Marukichi-kō|ja|丸吉講}}. Local traditional events are still celebrated by the descendants of the Fuji-kō.
Inside the ema hall, several votive plaques and pilgrimage pictures (ema) dating from the Edo period display realistic depictions of the shrine’s historical scenery.
== Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around June 15 (example: “Reidai-sai”) * Other annual events include Bon Odori in July, and a “Yamate no Fuji” opening event in August – the largest mound in the district, which has been designated as a tangible folk cultural property. * The “Nakazato Hayashi,” a traditional festival inherited from the Edo period, is still performed during the New Year at the shrine.
== Cultural Properties == === Tangible Folk Cultural Property (Fukuoka Prefecture Designation) === * Nakazato Fuji (Fujizuka): One of the larger mounds built by the Fuji-kō (a group of pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. It stands 12 meters tall (from the base on its south side) with a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument from Bunsei 5 (1822) indicates it existed during the Edo period. Among the 36 stone structures, the monument of the Dōso-gami is unique in the district.
=== Treasures === * Ema (votive plaques) commemorating the subjugation of the eight‐headed serpent (dating from Tenpō 5, January) * Ema commemorating Mt. Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March)
== Access == * From the [[Seibu Ikebukuro Line]] at {{ill|Hayamachi Station|ja|保谷駅}} South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[Toei Oedo Line]] at {{ill|Hikarigaoka Station|ja|光が丘駅}}, take a bus for 19 minutes.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Volume 134, Shinza District No.6|chapter=Hashido Village Tennōsha|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village Tennōsha}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.miyama.lg.jp/info/prev.asp?fol_id=2168 Ema and Great Lanterns] (Miyama City “Cultural Properties”)

{{draft categories| [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Nerima Ward Shrines]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Properties of Municipalities]] [[Category:Fujizuka]] [[Category:mass translations]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E5%9D%82%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%B7%B4%E9%A6%AC%E5%8C%BA%E5%A4%A7%E6%B3%89%E7%94%BA)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District)}}

revision.comment: Bot: Replaced ill templates with appended |12=draft and created corresponding redirect pages.
revision.text:
{{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | native_name = {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} | image = [[File:Yasaka Jinja Nerima.jpg|300px]] | image description = Main Hall of Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都|12=draft}} {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区|12=draft}} {{ill|Oizumi-cho|ja|大泉町|12=draft}} | address = 1-44-1, Oizumi-cho | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=draft}} | temple_ranking = (if any; here given as "Village Shrine") | founded = | subsidiary shrines = {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mikudate Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=draft}} | alternate name = | festival = Main festival (example: Grand Festival around June 15) | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|2|N|139|36|22|E}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社|12=draft}}) in Oizumi-cho, Honma District is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Oizumi-cho, Nerima Ward, Tokyo. It is a village shrine and is famed for legends concerning a curse for those who eat melons, due to its association with the “Gion” tradition.
== Deity == The primary deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=draft}}. In addition, three subsidiary deities enshrined in the subsidiary shrines are: * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|稲蒼魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} (at the Inari Shrine) * {{ill|Konohana Sakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft}} (at the Asama Shrine) * {{ill|Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|ja|国常立命|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|12=draft}} (at the Mikudate Shrine)
== History == Its founding date is unknown. The {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵国風土記稿|12=draft}} also records that “the history of the shrine’s establishment is unknown” (see also {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village, Tennōsha}}). In that text it is also referred to as “Tennōsha.” The name was changed to Yasaka Shrine in the early Meiji period due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=draft}}.
== Grounds == An image of a “Fujizuka” (a mound resembling Mt. Fuji) known as “Nakazato Fuji” stands on the right-hand side of the shrine’s main hall. This mound was constructed by the local 【ill|Fuji-kō|ja|富士講】 group from the Edo period to early Showa period. A stone monument dated to 1822 ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政|12=draft}} 5) within the mound suggests that its construction began in the Edo period and was later completed in the early Meiji period by the local {{ill|Marukichi-kō|ja|丸吉講|12=draft}}. Local traditional events are still celebrated by the descendants of the Fuji-kō.
Inside the ema hall, several votive plaques and pilgrimage pictures (ema) dating from the Edo period display realistic depictions of the shrine’s historical scenery.
== Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around June 15 (example: “Reidai-sai”) * Other annual events include Bon Odori in July, and a “Yamate no Fuji” opening event in August – the largest mound in the district, which has been designated as a tangible folk cultural property. * The “Nakazato Hayashi,” a traditional festival inherited from the Edo period, is still performed during the New Year at the shrine.
== Cultural Properties == === Tangible Folk Cultural Property (Fukuoka Prefecture Designation) === * Nakazato Fuji (Fujizuka): One of the larger mounds built by the Fuji-kō (a group of pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. It stands 12 meters tall (from the base on its south side) with a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument from Bunsei 5 (1822) indicates it existed during the Edo period. Among the 36 stone structures, the monument of the Dōso-gami is unique in the district.
=== Treasures === * Ema (votive plaques) commemorating the subjugation of the eight‐headed serpent (dating from Tenpō 5, January) * Ema commemorating Mt. Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March)
== Access == * From the [[Seibu Ikebukuro Line]] at {{ill|Hayamachi Station|ja|保谷駅|12=draft}} South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|12=draft}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[Toei Oedo Line]] at {{ill|Hikarigaoka Station|ja|光が丘駅|12=draft}}, take a bus for 19 minutes.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Volume 134, Shinza District No.6|chapter=Hashido Village Tennōsha|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village Tennōsha}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.miyama.lg.jp/info/prev.asp?fol_id=2168 Ema and Great Lanterns] (Miyama City “Cultural Properties”)
{{shinto-stub}}

33 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町) up to rev 97899421
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 八坂神社 |画像 = [[File:Yasaka Jinja Nerima.jpg|300px|]]
八坂神社の本殿（練馬区大泉町） |所在地 = [[東京都]][[練馬区]][[大泉町 (練馬区)|大泉町]]1-44-1 |祭神 = [[スサノオノミコト|須佐之男命]] |祭儀   = |社殿 = 本殿 幣殿 拝殿(木造) |創建 = |境内神社= 稲荷神社、浅間神社、御岳神社 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 例大祭 6月15日{{Cite book|和書|author=練馬古文書研究会|title=練馬ふるさと事典|publisher=松林孝至|year=2011|page=246}} |神事 = |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|2|N|139|36|22|E}} |地図 = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|46|2.37|N|139|36|22.41|E}}}} }} '''八坂神社'''（やさかじんじゃ）は、東京都練馬区大泉町にある[[神社]]{{Cite book|和書|author=東京都神社庁|title=東京都神社名鑑 上巻|publisher=東京都神社庁|year=1986|page=662}}。境内神社として、稲荷神社、浅間神社、御嶽神社がある。[[八坂神社]]は五瓜紋のため瓜を嫌うという謂れがあり、食べた者は神罰を受けるという伝説が残っている{{Cite book|和書|author=練馬区立石神井公園ふるさと文化館|title=ねりまの昔ばなし|publisher=練馬区立石神井公園ふるさと文化館|year=2011-2-25|page=98|date=}}。 == 祭神 == 主祭神は須佐之男命（[[スサノオ]]）。以下の3柱は境内神社にあり、総称して副祭神と呼ばれる。 *[[ウカノミタマ|稲蒼魂命]] - （稲荷神社の神）{{Cite book|和書|author=東京都神社庁 |title=東京都神社名鑑　上巻 |publisher=東京都神社庁 |year=1986 |page=628 |isbn=}} 　 *[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花開耶姫命]] - （浅間神社の神） *[[国之常立神|国常立命]] - （御嶽神社の神） ==歴史== 創立年代不明。[[文化 (元号)|文化]]・[[文政]]期に編まれた『[[新編武蔵風土記稿|新編武蔵国風土記稿]]』においても「社殿鎮座年歴不明」とある{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 橋戸村 天王社}}。また、同書では「天王社」と記されており{{Cite book|和書|author=練馬区教育委員会 |title=練馬の神社 |publisher=練馬区教育委員会 |year=1985-3 |page=36 |isbn=}}{{Cite book|和書|author=大泉農協年史編纂委員会 |title=大泉農業協同組合40年史 |publisher=大泉農業協同組合 |year=1990-9 |page=718 |isbn=}}{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 橋戸村 天王社}}、現在の社名である八坂神社に改められたのは明治初年である{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai21_30.files/dai30.pdf |title=ねりまの文化財　第30号 |publisher =練馬区教育委員会社会教育課 |accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}{{Cite book|和書|author=練馬区教育委員会 |title=練馬を往く |publisher=練馬区教育委員会 |year=2000-3 |page=53 |isbn=}}。 ==境内== [[ファイル:練馬区大泉_八坂神社_中里の富士塚.jpg|代替文=練馬区大泉 八坂神社 中里の富士塚|サムネイル|[[:ファイル:練馬区大泉 八坂神社 中里の富士塚.jpg|中里の富士塚]]]] 本殿に向かう石造りの鳥居をくぐると、参道両側は深い緑樹が続いていく。境内が一段と高くなった正面本殿の右手前に額堂がある。これは以前は左側にあったが、右側に移転された。正面は拝殿、その奥に神殿がある。社殿の右側に、「中里富士」と呼ばれる富士山に似せた大富士塚があり、これは江戸時代から昭和初期にかけて地域の[[富士講]]の人々によって築造された{{Cite book|和書|author=練馬区地域文化部文化・生涯学習課伝統文化係 |title=練馬区の文化財 指定文化財編 |year=2016|page=10}}。富士塚内に[[1822年]]（[[文政]]5年）の石碑があることから、江戸時代に築造が開始されたものと思われ、明治初期の地元の[[丸吉講]]によって完成したと伝わっている。現代でも区内の富士講の人々によって伝統行事が受け継がれている。 絵馬堂には、参詣図などの絵馬が十数枚奉納されている。参詣図は江戸時代のもので、当時の景観が写実的に描かれている{{Cite book|和書|author=練馬区教育委員会 |title=練馬を往く |publisher=練馬区教育委員会 |year=2000-3 |page=49}}。 === 境内社 === 境内末社には、稲荷神社(祭神　稲蒼魂命)、浅間神社(祭神　木木花咲耶姫)、御嶽神社(祭神　国常立命)がある。 稲荷神社、浅間神社は本殿の左側、御嶽神社は左側にある。 == 年中行事 == * 6月15日 - 例大祭 * 7月 - 盆踊り{{Cite web|和書|title=八坂神社 納涼盆踊り大会 -2019年- [祭の日]|url=https://matsuri-no-hi.com/matsuri/1235|website=祭の日|accessdate=2019-11-03|language=ja}} * 8月 - 中里富士の山開き - 区では一番大きい塚となっている中里富士。区の[[民俗文化財|有形民俗文化財]]に指定されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nerimakanko.jp/event/annual.php |title= 年間イベントガイド |publisher =練馬区産業振興公社 |accessdate=2019-11-02}}。 * 江戸時代から「中里ばやし」が継承されている。大祭、正月に神前に奉納している{{Cite web|和書|title=お囃子 （おはやし）|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/bunkazai/bhayashi.html|website=練馬区公式ホームページ|accessdate=2019-11-03}}。 == 文化財 == === 有形民俗文化財 === * 中里の富士塚（なかざとのふじづか） 江戸時代から昭和初期にかけて盛んだった、富士山登拝者の集団である[[富士講]]が築いた[[富士塚]]の一つ。練馬区内でも比較的大きなもので、高さは南側基部から12メートル、径は30メートルある。明治初期に丸吉講により築かれたとされるが、文政5年（1822年）の石碑から江戸時代にはすでに存在したと考えられる。36基の石造物の中でも道祖神の碑は区内で唯一のものとされる{{Cite web|和書|title=中里の富士塚（なかざとのふじづか）|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/bunkazai/b017.html|website=：練馬区公式ホームページ|accessdate=2019-11-02}}。 === 社宝 === * 絵馬（八岐大蛇退治、[[天保]]5年7月） * 絵馬（富士登山記念、[[寛永]]7年6月） * 絵馬（伊勢参宮図、中里白石藤五郎・村田増太郎による、寛永3年3月）{{Cite book|和書|author=石神井図書館郷土資料室|title=練馬の絵馬|publisher=練馬区教育委員会|year=1978-8-15|page=175、178|isbn=|date=}} ==交通アクセス== *[[西武池袋線]][[保谷駅]]南口より[[みどりバス]]で「別荘橋」{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/nerimanokonjaku/kawahashikoen/bessobashi.html |title=別荘橋 |publisher =練馬区公式ホームページ |accessdate=2019-11-02}}バス停下車徒歩3分 *[[都営地下鉄大江戸線]][[光が丘駅]]よりバス19分 ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=巻ノ134新座郡ノ6|chapter=橋戸村 天王社|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 橋戸村 天王社}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やさかしんしや ねりまくおおいすみちよう}} [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:練馬区の神社]] [[Category:区市町村指定有形民俗文化財]] [[Category:富士塚]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)]] to [[Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

（稲荷神社の神）{{Cite book|和書|author=東京都神社庁 |title=東京都神社名鑑　上巻 |publisher=東京都神社庁 |year=1986 |page=628 |isbn=}} 　 *[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花開耶姫命]] - （浅間神社の神） *[[国之常立神|国常立命]] - （御嶽神社の神） ==歴史== 創立年代不明。[[文化 (元号)|文化]]・[[文政]]期に編まれた『[[新編武蔵風土記稿|新編武蔵国風土記稿]]』においても「社殿鎮座年歴不明」とある{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 橋戸村 天王社}}。また、同書では「天王社」と記されており{{Cite book|和書|author=練馬区教育委員会 |title=練馬の神社 |publisher=練馬区教育委員会 |year=1985-3 |page=36 |isbn=}}{{Cite book|和書|author=大泉農協年史編纂委員会 |title=大泉農業協同組合40年史 |publisher=大泉農業協同組合 |year=1990-9 |page=718 |isbn=}}{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 橋戸村 天王社}}、現在の社名である八坂神社に改められたのは明治初年である{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai21_30.files/dai30.pdf |title=ねりまの文化財　第30号 |publisher =練馬区教育委員会社会教育課 |accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}{{Cite book|和書|author=練馬区教育委員会 |title=練馬を往く |publisher=練馬区教育委員会 |year=2000-3 |page=53 |isbn=}}。 ==境内== [[ファイル:練馬区大泉_八坂神社_中里の富士塚.jpg|代替文=練馬区大泉 八坂神社 中里の富士塚|サムネイル|[[:ファイル:練馬区大泉 八坂神社 中里の富士塚.jpg|中里の富士塚]]]] 本殿に向かう石造りの鳥居をくぐると、参道両側は深い緑樹が続いていく。境内が一段と高くなった正面本殿の右手前に額堂がある。これは以前は左側にあったが、右側に移転された。正面は拝殿、その奥に神殿がある。社殿の右側に、「中里富士」と呼ばれる富士山に似せた大富士塚があり、これは江戸時代から昭和初期にかけて地域の[[富士講]]の人々によって築造された{{Cite book|和書|author=練馬区地域文化部文化・生涯学習課伝統文化係 |title=練馬区の文化財 指定文化財編 |year=2016|page=10}}。富士塚内に[[1822年]]（[[文政]]5年）の石碑があることから、江戸時代に築造が開始されたものと思われ、明治初期の地元の[[丸吉講]]によって完成したと伝わっている。現代でも区内の富士講の人々によって伝統行事が受け継がれている。 絵馬堂には、参詣図などの絵馬が十数枚奉納されている。参詣図は江戸時代のもので、当時の景観が写実的に描かれている{{Cite book|和書|author=練馬区教育委員会 |title=練馬を往く |publisher=練馬区教育委員会 |year=2000-3 |page=49}}。 === 境内社 === 境内末社には、稲荷神社(祭神　稲蒼魂命)、浅間神社(祭神　木木花咲耶姫)、御嶽神社(祭神　国常立命)がある。 稲荷神社、浅間神社は本殿の左側、御嶽神社は左側にある。 == 年中行事 == * 6月15日 - 例大祭 * 7月 - 盆踊り{{Cite web|和書|title=八坂神社 納涼盆踊り大会 -2019年- [祭の日]|url=https://matsuri-no-hi.com/matsuri/1235|website=祭の日|accessdate=2019-11-03|language=ja}} * 8月 - 中里富士の山開き - 区では一番大きい塚となっている中里富士。区の[[民俗文化財|有形民俗文化財]]に指定されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nerimakanko.jp/event/annual.php |title= 年間イベントガイド |publisher =練馬区産業振興公社 |accessdate=2019-11-02}}。 * 江戸時代から「中里ばやし」が継承されている。大祭、正月に神前に奉納している{{Cite web|和書|title=お囃子 （おはやし）|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/bunkazai/bhayashi.html|website=練馬区公式ホームページ|accessdate=2019-11-03}}。 == 文化財 == === 有形民俗文化財 === * 中里の富士塚（なかざとのふじづか） 江戸時代から昭和初期にかけて盛んだった、富士山登拝者の集団である[[富士講]]が築いた[[富士塚]]の一つ。練馬区内でも比較的大きなもので、高さは南側基部から12メートル、径は30メートルある。明治初期に丸吉講により築かれたとされるが、文政5年（1822年）の石碑から江戸時代にはすでに存在したと考えられる。36基の石造物の中でも道祖神の碑は区内で唯一のものとされる{{Cite web|和書|title=中里の富士塚（なかざとのふじづか）|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/bunkazai/b017.html|website=：練馬区公式ホームページ|accessdate=2019-11-02}}。 === 社宝 === * 絵馬（八岐大蛇退治、[[天保]]5年7月） * 絵馬（富士登山記念、[[寛永]]7年6月） * 絵馬（伊勢参宮図、中里白石藤五郎・村田増太郎による、寛永3年3月）{{Cite book|和書|author=石神井図書館郷土資料室|title=練馬の絵馬|publisher=練馬区教育委員会|year=1978-8-15|page=175、178|isbn=|date=}} ==交通アクセス== *[[西武池袋線]][[保谷駅]]南口より[[みどりバス]]で「別荘橋」{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/nerimanokonjaku/kawahashikoen/bessobashi.html |title=別荘橋 |publisher =練馬区公式ホームページ |accessdate=2019-11-02}}バス停下車徒歩3分 *[[都営地下鉄大江戸線]][[光が丘駅]]よりバス19分 ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=巻ノ134新座郡ノ6|chapter=橋戸村 天王社|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 橋戸村 天王社}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やさかしんしや ねりまくおおいすみちよう}} [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:練馬区の神社]] [[Category:区市町村指定有形民俗文化財]] [[Category:富士塚]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Oizumi Yasaka jinja]][[ja:八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q85876838]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | native_name = [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = File:Yasaka Jinja Nerima.jpg | image description = Main Hall of Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]] [[:en:Ōizumi, Gunma|Oizumi-cho]] | address = 1-44-1, Oizumi-cho | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}} | temple_Ranking = (if any; here given as "Village Shrine") | founded = | subsidiary shrines = [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]], [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]], {{ill|Mikudate Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikudate Shrine|qq=|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} | alternate name = | festival = Main festival (example: Grand Festival around June 15) | coordinates = {{Coord|35.76722|N|139.60611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Nerima-ku|qid=Q232655}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ([[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]]) in Oizumi-cho, Honma District is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in Oizumi-cho, Nerima Ward, Tokyo. It is a village shrine and is famed for legends concerning a curse for those who eat melons, due to its association with the “Gion” tradition. == Deity == The primary deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}. In addition, three subsidiary deities enshrined in the subsidiary shrines are: * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|稲蒼魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ウカノミタマ}} (at the Inari Shrine) * {{ill|Konohana Sakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohana Sakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (at the Asama Shrine) * {{ill|Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|ja|国常立命|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Tokotachi|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=国之常立神}} (at the Mikudate Shrine) == History == Its founding date is unknown. The {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵国風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=新編武蔵風土記稿|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} also records that “the history of the shrine’s establishment is unknown” (see also {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village, Tennōsha}}). In that text it is also referred to as “Tennōsha.” The name was changed to Yasaka Shrine in the early Meiji period due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}. == Grounds == An image of a “Fujizuka” (a mound resembling Mt. Fuji) known as “Nakazato Fuji” stands on the right-hand side of the shrine’s main hall. This mound was constructed by the local 【ill|Fuji-kō|ja|富士講】 group from the Edo period to early Showa period. A stone monument dated to 1822 ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 5) within the mound suggests that its construction began in the Edo period and was later completed in the early Meiji period by the local {{ill|Marukichi-kō|ja|丸吉講|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marukichi-kō|qq=|1=Marukichi-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368055}}. Local traditional events are still celebrated by the descendants of the Fuji-kō. Inside the ema hall, several votive plaques and pilgrimage pictures (ema) dating from the Edo period display realistic depictions of the shrine’s historical scenery. == Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around June 15 (example: “Reidai-sai”) * Other annual events include Bon Odori in July, and a “Yamate no Fuji” opening event in August – the largest mound in the district, which has been designated as a tangible folk cultural property. * The “Nakazato Hayashi,” a traditional festival inherited from the Edo period, is still performed during the New Year at the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Folk Cultural Property (Fukuoka Prefecture Designation) === * Nakazato Fuji (Fujizuka): One of the larger mounds built by the Fuji-kō (a group of pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. It stands 12 meters tall (from the base on its south side) with a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument from Bunsei 5 (1822) indicates it existed during the Edo period. Among the 36 stone structures, the monument of the Dōso-gami is unique in the district. === Treasures === * Ema (votive plaques) commemorating the subjugation of the eight‐headed serpent (dating from Tenpō 5, January) * Ema commemorating Mt. Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March) == Access == * From the [[:en:Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Seibu Ikebukuro Line]] at [[:en:Hōya Station|Hayamachi Station]] South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Bus|qq=|1=Midori Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279068}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[:en:Toei Oedo Line|Toei Oedo Line]] at [[:en:Hikarigaoka Station|Hikarigaoka Station]], take a bus for 19 minutes. == References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Volume 134, Shinza District No.6|chapter=Hashido Village Tennōsha|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village Tennōsha}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]
== External Links == * [http://www.city.miyama.lg.jp/info/prev.asp?fol_id=2168 Ema and Great Lanterns] (Miyama City “Cultural Properties”) {{shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)|version=97899421|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Completion tier 1]] }}

Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March) == Access == * From the [[:en:Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Seibu Ikebukuro Line]] at [[:en:Hōya Station|Hayamachi Station]] South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Bus|qq=|1=Midori Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279068}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[:en:Toei Oedo Line|Toei Oedo Line]] at [[:en:Hikarigaoka Station|Hikarigaoka Station]], take a bus for 19 minutes. == References ==

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Oizumi Yasaka jinja]][[ja:八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q85876838]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | native_name = [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = File:Yasaka Jinja Nerima.jpg | image description = Main Hall of Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]] [[:en:Ōizumi, Gunma|Oizumi-cho]] | address = 1-44-1, Oizumi-cho | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}} | temple_Ranking = (if any; here given as "Village Shrine") | founded = | subsidiary shrines = [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]], [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]], {{ill|Mikudate Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikudate Shrine|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} | alternate name = | festival = Main festival (example: Grand Festival around June 15) | coordinates = {{Coord|35.76722|N|139.60611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Nerima-ku|WD=Q232655}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ([[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]]) in Oizumi-cho, Honma District is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in Oizumi-cho, Nerima Ward, Tokyo. It is a village shrine and is famed for legends concerning a curse for those who eat melons, due to its association with the “Gion” tradition. == Deity == The primary deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}. In addition, three subsidiary deities enshrined in the subsidiary shrines are: * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|稲蒼魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ウカノミタマ}} (at the Inari Shrine) * {{ill|Konohana Sakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohana Sakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (at the Asama Shrine) * {{ill|Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|ja|国常立命|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=国之常立神}} (at the Mikudate Shrine) == History == Its founding date is unknown. The {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵国風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=新編武蔵風土記稿|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} also records that “the history of the shrine’s establishment is unknown” (see also {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village, Tennōsha}}). In that text it is also referred to as “Tennōsha.” The name was changed to Yasaka Shrine in the early Meiji period due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}. == Grounds == An image of a “Fujizuka” (a mound resembling Mt. Fuji) known as “Nakazato Fuji” stands on the right-hand side of the shrine’s main hall. This mound was constructed by the local 【ill|Fuji-kō|ja|富士講】 group from the Edo period to early Showa period. A stone monument dated to 1822 ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 5) within the mound suggests that its construction began in the Edo period and was later completed in the early Meiji period by the local {{ill|Marukichi-kō|ja|丸吉講|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marukichi-kō|1=Marukichi-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368055}}. Local traditional events are still celebrated by the descendants of the Fuji-kō. Inside the ema hall, several votive plaques and pilgrimage pictures (ema) dating from the Edo period display realistic depictions of the shrine’s historical scenery. == Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around June 15 (example: “Reidai-sai”) * Other annual events include Bon Odori in July, and a “Yamate no Fuji” opening event in August – the largest mound in the district, which has been designated as a tangible folk cultural property. * The “Nakazato Hayashi,” a traditional festival inherited from the Edo period, is still performed during the New Year at the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Folk Cultural Property (Fukuoka Prefecture Designation) === * Nakazato Fuji (Fujizuka): One of the larger mounds built by the Fuji-kō (a group of pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. It stands 12 meters tall (from the base on its south side) with a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument from Bunsei 5 (1822) indicates it existed during the Edo period. Among the 36 stone structures, the monument of the Dōso-gami is unique in the district. === Treasures === * Ema (votive plaques) commemorating the subjugation of the eight‐headed serpent (dating from Tenpō 5, January) * Ema commemorating Mt. Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March) == Access == * From the [[:en:Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Seibu Ikebukuro Line]] at [[:en:Hōya Station|Hayamachi Station]] South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Bus|1=Midori Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279068}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[:en:Toei Oedo Line|Toei Oedo Line]] at [[:en:Hikarigaoka Station|Hikarigaoka Station]], take a bus for 19 minutes. == References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Volume 134, Shinza District No.6|chapter=Hashido Village Tennōsha|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village Tennōsha}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]
== External Links == * [http://www.city.miyama.lg.jp/info/prev.asp?fol_id=2168 Ema and Great Lanterns] (Miyama City “Cultural Properties”) {{shinto-stub}}

Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March) == Access == * From the [[:en:Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Seibu Ikebukuro Line]] at [[:en:Hōya Station|Hayamachi Station]] South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Bus|1=Midori Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279068}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[:en:Toei Oedo Line|Toei Oedo Line]] at [[:en:Hikarigaoka Station|Hikarigaoka Station]], take a bus for 19 minutes. == References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Volume 134, Shinza District No.6|chapter=Hashido Village Tennōsha|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village Tennōsha}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Oizumi Yasaka jinja]][[ja:八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q85876838]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | native_name = [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = File:Yasaka Jinja Nerima.jpg | image description = Main Hall of Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]] [[:en:Ōizumi, Gunma|Oizumi-cho]] | address = 1-44-1, Oizumi-cho | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}} | temple_Ranking = (if any; here given as "Village Shrine") | founded = | subsidiary shrines = [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]], [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]], {{ill|Mikudate Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikudate Shrine|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} | alternate name = | festival = Main festival (example: Grand Festival around June 15) | coordinates = {{Coord|35.76722|N|139.60611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Nerima-ku|WD=Q232655}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ([[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]]) in Oizumi-cho, Honma District is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in Oizumi-cho, Nerima Ward, Tokyo. It is a village shrine and is famed for legends concerning a curse for those who eat melons, due to its association with the “Gion” tradition. == Deity == The primary deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}. In addition, three subsidiary deities enshrined in the subsidiary shrines are: * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|稲蒼魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ウカノミタマ}} (at the Inari Shrine) * {{ill|Konohana Sakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohana Sakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (at the Asama Shrine) * {{ill|Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|ja|国常立命|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=国之常立神}} (at the Mikudate Shrine) == History == Its founding date is unknown. The {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵国風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=新編武蔵風土記稿|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} also records that “the history of the shrine’s establishment is unknown” (see also {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village, Tennōsha}}). In that text it is also referred to as “Tennōsha.” The name was changed to Yasaka Shrine in the early Meiji period due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}. == Grounds == An image of a “Fujizuka” (a mound resembling Mt. Fuji) known as “Nakazato Fuji” stands on the right-hand side of the shrine’s main hall. This mound was constructed by the local 【ill|Fuji-kō|ja|富士講】 group from the Edo period to early Showa period. A stone monument dated to 1822 ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 5) within the mound suggests that its construction began in the Edo period and was later completed in the early Meiji period by the local {{ill|Marukichi-kō|ja|丸吉講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marukichi-kō|1=Marukichi-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368055}}. Local traditional events are still celebrated by the descendants of the Fuji-kō. Inside the ema hall, several votive plaques and pilgrimage pictures (ema) dating from the Edo period display realistic depictions of the shrine’s historical scenery. == Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around June 15 (example: “Reidai-sai”) * Other annual events include Bon Odori in July, and a “Yamate no Fuji” opening event in August – the largest mound in the district, which has been designated as a tangible folk cultural property. * The “Nakazato Hayashi,” a traditional festival inherited from the Edo period, is still performed during the New Year at the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Folk Cultural Property (Fukuoka Prefecture Designation) === * Nakazato Fuji (Fujizuka): One of the larger mounds built by the Fuji-kō (a group of pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. It stands 12 meters tall (from the base on its south side) with a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument from Bunsei 5 (1822) indicates it existed during the Edo period. Among the 36 stone structures, the monument of the Dōso-gami is unique in the district. === Treasures === * Ema (votive plaques) commemorating the subjugation of the eight‐headed serpent (dating from Tenpō 5, January) * Ema commemorating Mt. Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March) == Access == * From the [[:en:Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Seibu Ikebukuro Line]] at [[:en:Hōya Station|Hayamachi Station]] South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Bus|1=Midori Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279068}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[:en:Toei Oedo Line|Toei Oedo Line]] at [[:en:Hikarigaoka Station|Hikarigaoka Station]], take a bus for 19 minutes. == References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Volume 134, Shinza District No.6|chapter=Hashido Village Tennōsha|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village Tennōsha}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]
== External Links == * [http://www.city.miyama.lg.jp/info/prev.asp?fol_id=2168 Ema and Great Lanterns] (Miyama City “Cultural Properties”) {{shinto-stub}}

Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March) == Access == * From the [[:en:Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Seibu Ikebukuro Line]] at [[:en:Hōya Station|Hayamachi Station]] South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Bus|1=Midori Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279068}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[:en:Toei Oedo Line|Toei Oedo Line]] at [[:en:Hikarigaoka Station|Hikarigaoka Station]], take a bus for 19 minutes. == References ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Oizumi Yasaka jinja]][[ja:八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q85876838]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | native_name = [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = File:Yasaka Jinja Nerima.jpg | image description = Main Hall of Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]] [[:en:Ōizumi, Gunma|Oizumi-cho]] | address = 1-44-1, Oizumi-cho | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}} | temple_Ranking = (if any; here given as "Village Shrine") | founded = | subsidiary shrines = [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]], [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]], {{ill|Mikudate Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikudate Shrine|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} | alternate name = | festival = Main festival (example: Grand Festival around June 15) | coordinates = {{Coord|35.76722|N|139.60611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Nerima-ku|WD=Q232655}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ([[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]]) in Oizumi-cho, Honma District is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in Oizumi-cho, Nerima Ward, Tokyo. It is a village shrine and is famed for legends concerning a curse for those who eat melons, due to its association with the “Gion” tradition. == Deity == The primary deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}. In addition, three subsidiary deities enshrined in the subsidiary shrines are: * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|稲蒼魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ウカノミタマ}} (at the Inari Shrine) * {{ill|Konohana Sakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohana Sakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (at the Asama Shrine) * {{ill|Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|ja|国常立命|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=国之常立神}} (at the Mikudate Shrine) == History == Its founding date is unknown. The {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵国風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=新編武蔵風土記稿|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} also records that “the history of the shrine’s establishment is unknown” (see also {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village, Tennōsha}}). In that text it is also referred to as “Tennōsha.” The name was changed to Yasaka Shrine in the early Meiji period due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}. == Grounds == An image of a “Fujizuka” (a mound resembling Mt. Fuji) known as “Nakazato Fuji” stands on the right-hand side of the shrine’s main hall. This mound was constructed by the local 【ill|Fuji-kō|ja|富士講】 group from the Edo period to early Showa period. A stone monument dated to 1822 ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 5) within the mound suggests that its construction began in the Edo period and was later completed in the early Meiji period by the local {{ill|Marukichi-kō|ja|丸吉講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marukichi-kō|1=Marukichi-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368055}}. Local traditional events are still celebrated by the descendants of the Fuji-kō. Inside the ema hall, several votive plaques and pilgrimage pictures (ema) dating from the Edo period display realistic depictions of the shrine’s historical scenery. == Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around June 15 (example: “Reidai-sai”) * Other annual events include Bon Odori in July, and a “Yamate no Fuji” opening event in August – the largest mound in the district, which has been designated as a tangible folk cultural property. * The “Nakazato Hayashi,” a traditional festival inherited from the Edo period, is still performed during the New Year at the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Folk Cultural Property (Fukuoka Prefecture Designation) === * Nakazato Fuji (Fujizuka): One of the larger mounds built by the Fuji-kō (a group of pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. It stands 12 meters tall (from the base on its south side) with a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument from Bunsei 5 (1822) indicates it existed during the Edo period. Among the 36 stone structures, the monument of the Dōso-gami is unique in the district. === Treasures === * Ema (votive plaques) commemorating the subjugation of the eight‐headed serpent (dating from Tenpō 5, January) * Ema commemorating Mt. Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March) == Access == * From the [[:en:Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Seibu Ikebukuro Line]] at [[:en:Hōya Station|Hayamachi Station]] South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Bus|1=Midori Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279068}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[:en:Toei Oedo Line|Toei Oedo Line]] at [[:en:Hikarigaoka Station|Hikarigaoka Station]], take a bus for 19 minutes. == References ==
{{Reflist}}

Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March) == Access == * From the [[:en:Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Seibu Ikebukuro Line]] at [[:en:Hōya Station|Hayamachi Station]] South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Bus|1=Midori Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279068}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[:en:Toei Oedo Line|Toei Oedo Line]] at [[:en:Hikarigaoka Station|Hikarigaoka Station]], take a bus for 19 minutes. == References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Volume 134, Shinza District No.6|chapter=Hashido Village Tennōsha|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village Tennōsha}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]
== External Links == * [http://www.city.miyama.lg.jp/info/prev.asp?fol_id=2168 Ema and Great Lanterns] (Miyama City “Cultural Properties”) {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Oizumi Yasaka jinja]][[ja:八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q85876838]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | native_name = [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = File:Yasaka Jinja Nerima.jpg | image description = Main Hall of Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]] [[:en:Ōizumi, Gunma|Oizumi-cho]] | address = 1-44-1, Oizumi-cho | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}} | temple_Ranking = (if any; here given as "Village Shrine") | founded = | subsidiary shrines = [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]], [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]], {{ill|Mikudate Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikudate Shrine|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} | alternate name = | festival = Main festival (example: Grand Festival around June 15) | coordinates = {{Coord|35.76722|N|139.60611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Nerima-ku|WD=Q232655}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ([[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]]) in Oizumi-cho, Honma District is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in Oizumi-cho, Nerima Ward, Tokyo. It is a village shrine and is famed for legends concerning a curse for those who eat melons, due to its association with the “Gion” tradition. == Deity == The primary deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}. In addition, three subsidiary deities enshrined in the subsidiary shrines are: * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|稲蒼魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ウカノミタマ}} (at the Inari Shrine) * {{ill|Konohana Sakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohana Sakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (at the Asama Shrine) * {{ill|Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|ja|国常立命|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=国之常立神}} (at the Mikudate Shrine) == History == Its founding date is unknown. The {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵国風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=新編武蔵風土記稿|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} also records that “the history of the shrine’s establishment is unknown” (see also {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village, Tennōsha}}). In that text it is also referred to as “Tennōsha.” The name was changed to Yasaka Shrine in the early Meiji period due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}. == Grounds == An image of a “Fujizuka” (a mound resembling Mt. Fuji) known as “Nakazato Fuji” stands on the right-hand side of the shrine’s main hall. This mound was constructed by the local 【ill|Fuji-kō|ja|富士講】 group from the Edo period to early Showa period. A stone monument dated to 1822 ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 5) within the mound suggests that its construction began in the Edo period and was later completed in the early Meiji period by the local {{ill|Marukichi-kō|ja|丸吉講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marukichi-kō|1=Marukichi-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368055}}. Local traditional events are still celebrated by the descendants of the Fuji-kō. Inside the ema hall, several votive plaques and pilgrimage pictures (ema) dating from the Edo period display realistic depictions of the shrine’s historical scenery. == Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around June 15 (example: “Reidai-sai”) * Other annual events include Bon Odori in July, and a “Yamate no Fuji” opening event in August – the largest mound in the district, which has been designated as a tangible folk cultural property. * The “Nakazato Hayashi,” a traditional festival inherited from the Edo period, is still performed during the New Year at the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Folk Cultural Property (Fukuoka Prefecture Designation) === * Nakazato Fuji (Fujizuka): One of the larger mounds built by the Fuji-kō (a group of pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. It stands 12 meters tall (from the base on its south side) with a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument from Bunsei 5 (1822) indicates it existed during the Edo period. Among the 36 stone structures, the monument of the Dōso-gami is unique in the district. === Treasures === * Ema (votive plaques) commemorating the subjugation of the eight‐headed serpent (dating from Tenpō 5, January) * Ema commemorating Mt. Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March) == Access == * From the [[:en:Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Seibu Ikebukuro Line]] at [[:en:Hōya Station|Hayamachi Station]] South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Bus|1=Midori Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279068}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[:en:Toei Oedo Line|Toei Oedo Line]] at [[:en:Hikarigaoka Station|Hikarigaoka Station]], take a bus for 19 minutes. == References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Volume 134, Shinza District No.6|chapter=Hashido Village Tennōsha|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village Tennōsha}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]
== External Links == * [http://www.city.miyama.lg.jp/info/prev.asp?fol_id=2168 Ema and Great Lanterns] (Miyama City “Cultural Properties”) {{shinto-stub}}

Local traditional events are still celebrated by the descendants of the Fuji-kō. Inside the ema hall, several votive plaques and pilgrimage pictures (ema) dating from the Edo period display realistic depictions of the shrine’s historical scenery. == Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around June 15 (example: “Reidai-sai”) * Other annual events include Bon Odori in July, and a “Yamate no Fuji” opening event in August – the largest mound in the district, which has been designated as a tangible folk cultural property. * The “Nakazato Hayashi,” a traditional festival inherited from the Edo period, is still performed during the New Year at the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Folk Cultural Property (Fukuoka Prefecture Designation) === * Nakazato Fuji (Fujizuka): One of the larger mounds built by the Fuji-kō (a group of pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. It stands 12 meters tall (from the base on its south side) with a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument from Bunsei 5 (1822) indicates it existed during the Edo period. Among the 36 stone structures, the monument of the Dōso-gami is unique in the district. === Treasures === * Ema (votive plaques) commemorating the subjugation of the eight‐headed serpent (dating from Tenpō 5, January) * Ema commemorating Mt. Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March) == Access == * From the [[:en:Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Seibu Ikebukuro Line]] at [[:en:Hōya Station|Hayamachi Station]] South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Bus|1=Midori Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279068}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[:en:Toei Oedo Line|Toei Oedo Line]] at [[:en:Hikarigaoka Station|Hikarigaoka Station]], take a bus for 19 minutes. == References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Volume 134, Shinza District No.6|chapter=Hashido Village Tennōsha|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village Tennōsha}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]
== External Links == * [http://www.city.miyama.lg.jp/info/prev.asp?fol_id=2168 Ema and Great Lanterns] (Miyama City “Cultural Properties”) {{shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)|version=97899421|comment=Imported full ja history}}
