<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Oizumi Yasaka jinja]][[ja:八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q85876838]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | native_name = [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = File:Yasaka Jinja Nerima.jpg | image description = Main Hall of Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]] [[:en:Ōizumi, Gunma|Oizumi-cho]] | address = 1-44-1, Oizumi-cho | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}} | temple_Ranking = (if any; here given as "Village Shrine") | founded = | subsidiary shrines = [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]], [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]], {{ill|Mikudate Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikudate Shrine|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} | alternate name = | festival = Main festival (example: Grand Festival around June 15) | coordinates = {{Coord|35.76722|N|139.60611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Nerima-ku|WD=Q232655}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ([[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]]) in Oizumi-cho, Honma District is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in Oizumi-cho, Nerima Ward, Tokyo. It is a village shrine and is famed for legends concerning a curse for those who eat melons, due to its association with the “Gion” tradition. == Deity == The primary deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}. In addition, three subsidiary deities enshrined in the subsidiary shrines are: * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|稲蒼魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ウカノミタマ}} (at the Inari Shrine) * {{ill|Konohana Sakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohana Sakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (at the Asama Shrine) * {{ill|Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|ja|国常立命|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=国之常立神}} (at the Mikudate Shrine) == History == Its founding date is unknown. The {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵国風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=新編武蔵風土記稿|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} also records that “the history of the shrine’s establishment is unknown” (see also {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village, Tennōsha}}). In that text it is also referred to as “Tennōsha.” The name was changed to Yasaka Shrine in the early Meiji period due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}. == Grounds == An image of a “Fujizuka” (a mound resembling Mt. Fuji) known as “Nakazato Fuji” stands on the right-hand side of the shrine’s main hall. This mound was constructed by the local 【ill|Fuji-kō|ja|富士講】 group from the Edo period to early Showa period. A stone monument dated to 1822 ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 5) within the mound suggests that its construction began in the Edo period and was later completed in the early Meiji period by the local {{ill|Marukichi-kō|ja|丸吉講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marukichi-kō|1=Marukichi-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368055}}. Local traditional events are still celebrated by the descendants of the Fuji-kō. Inside the ema hall, several votive plaques and pilgrimage pictures (ema) dating from the Edo period display realistic depictions of the shrine’s historical scenery. == Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around June 15 (example: “Reidai-sai”) * Other annual events include Bon Odori in July, and a “Yamate no Fuji” opening event in August – the largest mound in the district, which has been designated as a tangible folk cultural property. * The “Nakazato Hayashi,” a traditional festival inherited from the Edo period, is still performed during the New Year at the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Folk Cultural Property (Fukuoka Prefecture Designation) === * Nakazato Fuji (Fujizuka): One of the larger mounds built by the Fuji-kō (a group of pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. It stands 12 meters tall (from the base on its south side) with a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument from Bunsei 5 (1822) indicates it existed during the Edo period. Among the 36 stone structures, the monument of the Dōso-gami is unique in the district. === Treasures === * Ema (votive plaques) commemorating the subjugation of the eight‐headed serpent (dating from Tenpō 5, January) * Ema commemorating Mt. Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March) == Access == * From the [[:en:Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Seibu Ikebukuro Line]] at [[:en:Hōya Station|Hayamachi Station]] South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Bus|1=Midori Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279068}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[:en:Toei Oedo Line|Toei Oedo Line]] at [[:en:Hikarigaoka Station|Hikarigaoka Station]], take a bus for 19 minutes. == References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Volume 134, Shinza District No.6|chapter=Hashido Village Tennōsha|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village Tennōsha}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]
== External Links == * [http://www.city.miyama.lg.jp/info/prev.asp?fol_id=2168 Ema and Great Lanterns] (Miyama City “Cultural Properties”) {{shinto-stub}}

In addition, three subsidiary deities enshrined in the subsidiary shrines are: * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|稲蒼魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ウカノミタマ}} (at the Inari Shrine) * {{ill|Konohana Sakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohana Sakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (at the Asama Shrine) * {{ill|Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|ja|国常立命|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=国之常立神}} (at the Mikudate Shrine) == History == Its founding date is unknown. The {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵国風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=新編武蔵風土記稿|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} also records that “the history of the shrine’s establishment is unknown” (see also {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village, Tennōsha}}). In that text it is also referred to as “Tennōsha.” The name was changed to Yasaka Shrine in the early Meiji period due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}. == Grounds == An image of a “Fujizuka” (a mound resembling Mt. Fuji) known as “Nakazato Fuji” stands on the right-hand side of the shrine’s main hall. This mound was constructed by the local 【ill|Fuji-kō|ja|富士講】 group from the Edo period to early Showa period. A stone monument dated to 1822 ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 5) within the mound suggests that its construction began in the Edo period and was later completed in the early Meiji period by the local {{ill|Marukichi-kō|ja|丸吉講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marukichi-kō|1=Marukichi-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368055}}. Local traditional events are still celebrated by the descendants of the Fuji-kō. Inside the ema hall, several votive plaques and pilgrimage pictures (ema) dating from the Edo period display realistic depictions of the shrine’s historical scenery. == Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around June 15 (example: “Reidai-sai”) * Other annual events include Bon Odori in July, and a “Yamate no Fuji” opening event in August – the largest mound in the district, which has been designated as a tangible folk cultural property. * The “Nakazato Hayashi,” a traditional festival inherited from the Edo period, is still performed during the New Year at the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Folk Cultural Property (Fukuoka Prefecture Designation) === * Nakazato Fuji (Fujizuka): One of the larger mounds built by the Fuji-kō (a group of pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. It stands 12 meters tall (from the base on its south side) with a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument from Bunsei 5 (1822) indicates it existed during the Edo period. Among the 36 stone structures, the monument of the Dōso-gami is unique in the district. === Treasures === * Ema (votive plaques) commemorating the subjugation of the eight‐headed serpent (dating from Tenpō 5, January) * Ema commemorating Mt. Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March) == Access == * From the [[:en:Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Seibu Ikebukuro Line]] at [[:en:Hōya Station|Hayamachi Station]] South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Bus|1=Midori Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279068}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[:en:Toei Oedo Line|Toei Oedo Line]] at [[:en:Hikarigaoka Station|Hikarigaoka Station]], take a bus for 19 minutes. == References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Volume 134, Shinza District No.6|chapter=Hashido Village Tennōsha|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village Tennōsha}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]
== External Links == * [http://www.city.miyama.lg.jp/info/prev.asp?fol_id=2168 Ema and Great Lanterns] (Miyama City “Cultural Properties”) {{shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)|version=97899421|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is a village shrine and is famed for legends concerning a curse for those who eat melons, due to its association with the “Gion” tradition. == Deity == The primary deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}. In addition, three subsidiary deities enshrined in the subsidiary shrines are: * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|稲蒼魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ウカノミタマ}} (at the Inari Shrine) * {{ill|Konohana Sakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohana Sakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (at the Asama Shrine) * {{ill|Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|ja|国常立命|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=国之常立神}} (at the Mikudate Shrine) == History == Its founding date is unknown. The {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵国風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=新編武蔵風土記稿|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} also records that “the history of the shrine’s establishment is unknown” (see also {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village, Tennōsha}}). In that text it is also referred to as “Tennōsha.” The name was changed to Yasaka Shrine in the early Meiji period due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}. == Grounds == An image of a “Fujizuka” (a mound resembling Mt. Fuji) known as “Nakazato Fuji” stands on the right-hand side of the shrine’s main hall. This mound was constructed by the local 【ill|Fuji-kō|ja|富士講】 group from the Edo period to early Showa period. A stone monument dated to 1822 ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 5) within the mound suggests that its construction began in the Edo period and was later completed in the early Meiji period by the local {{ill|Marukichi-kō|ja|丸吉講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marukichi-kō|1=Marukichi-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368055}}. Local traditional events are still celebrated by the descendants of the Fuji-kō. Inside the ema hall, several votive plaques and pilgrimage pictures (ema) dating from the Edo period display realistic depictions of the shrine’s historical scenery. == Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around June 15 (example: “Reidai-sai”) * Other annual events include Bon Odori in July, and a “Yamate no Fuji” opening event in August – the largest mound in the district, which has been designated as a tangible folk cultural property. * The “Nakazato Hayashi,” a traditional festival inherited from the Edo period, is still performed during the New Year at the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Folk Cultural Property (Fukuoka Prefecture Designation) === * Nakazato Fuji (Fujizuka): One of the larger mounds built by the Fuji-kō (a group of pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. It stands 12 meters tall (from the base on its south side) with a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument from Bunsei 5 (1822) indicates it existed during the Edo period. Among the 36 stone structures, the monument of the Dōso-gami is unique in the district. === Treasures === * Ema (votive plaques) commemorating the subjugation of the eight‐headed serpent (dating from Tenpō 5, January) * Ema commemorating Mt. Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March) == Access == * From the [[:en:Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Seibu Ikebukuro Line]] at [[:en:Hōya Station|Hayamachi Station]] South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Bus|1=Midori Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279068}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[:en:Toei Oedo Line|Toei Oedo Line]] at [[:en:Hikarigaoka Station|Hikarigaoka Station]], take a bus for 19 minutes. == References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Volume 134, Shinza District No.6|chapter=Hashido Village Tennōsha|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village Tennōsha}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]
== External Links == * [http://www.city.miyama.lg.jp/info/prev.asp?fol_id=2168 Ema and Great Lanterns] (Miyama City “Cultural Properties”) {{shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)|version=97899421|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Completion tier 1]] }}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Oizumi Yasaka jinja]][[ja:八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Oizumi Yasaka jinja]][[ja:八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Oizumi Yasaka jinja]][[ja:八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q85876838]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | native_name = [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = File:Yasaka Jinja Nerima.jpg | image description = Main Hall of Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]] [[:en:Ōizumi, Gunma|Oizumi-cho]] | address = 1-44-1, Oizumi-cho | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}} | temple_Ranking = (if any; here given as "Village Shrine") | founded = | subsidiary shrines = [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]], [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]], {{ill|Mikudate Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikudate Shrine|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} | alternate name = | festival = Main festival (example: Grand Festival around June 15) | coordinates = {{Coord|35.76722|N|139.60611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Nerima-ku|WD=Q232655}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ([[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]]) in Oizumi-cho, Honma District is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in Oizumi-cho, Nerima Ward, Tokyo. It is a village shrine and is famed for legends concerning a curse for those who eat melons, due to its association with the “Gion” tradition. == Deity == The primary deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}. In addition, three subsidiary deities enshrined in the subsidiary shrines are: * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|稲蒼魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ウカノミタマ}} (at the Inari Shrine) * {{ill|Konohana Sakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohana Sakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (at the Asama Shrine) * {{ill|Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|ja|国常立命|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=国之常立神}} (at the Mikudate Shrine) == History == Its founding date is unknown. The {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵国風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=新編武蔵風土記稿|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} also records that “the history of the shrine’s establishment is unknown” (see also {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village, Tennōsha}}). In that text it is also referred to as “Tennōsha.” The name was changed to Yasaka Shrine in the early Meiji period due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}. == Grounds == An image of a “Fujizuka” (a mound resembling Mt. Fuji) known as “Nakazato Fuji” stands on the right-hand side of the shrine’s main hall. This mound was constructed by the local 【ill|Fuji-kō|ja|富士講】 group from the Edo period to early Showa period. A stone monument dated to 1822 ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 5) within the mound suggests that its construction began in the Edo period and was later completed in the early Meiji period by the local {{ill|Marukichi-kō|ja|丸吉講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marukichi-kō|1=Marukichi-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368055}}. Local traditional events are still celebrated by the descendants of the Fuji-kō. Inside the ema hall, several votive plaques and pilgrimage pictures (ema) dating from the Edo period display realistic depictions of the shrine’s historical scenery. == Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around June 15 (example: “Reidai-sai”) * Other annual events include Bon Odori in July, and a “Yamate no Fuji” opening event in August – the largest mound in the district, which has been designated as a tangible folk cultural property. * The “Nakazato Hayashi,” a traditional festival inherited from the Edo period, is still performed during the New Year at the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Folk Cultural Property (Fukuoka Prefecture Designation) === * Nakazato Fuji (Fujizuka): One of the larger mounds built by the Fuji-kō (a group of pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. It stands 12 meters tall (from the base on its south side) with a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument from Bunsei 5 (1822) indicates it existed during the Edo period. Among the 36 stone structures, the monument of the Dōso-gami is unique in the district. === Treasures === * Ema (votive plaques) commemorating the subjugation of the eight‐headed serpent (dating from Tenpō 5, January) * Ema commemorating Mt. Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March) == Access == * From the [[:en:Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Seibu Ikebukuro Line]] at [[:en:Hōya Station|Hayamachi Station]] South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Bus|1=Midori Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279068}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[:en:Toei Oedo Line|Toei Oedo Line]] at [[:en:Hikarigaoka Station|Hikarigaoka Station]], take a bus for 19 minutes. == References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Volume 134, Shinza District No.6|chapter=Hashido Village Tennōsha|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village Tennōsha}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]
== External Links == * [http://www.city.miyama.lg.jp/info/prev.asp?fol_id=2168 Ema and Great Lanterns] (Miyama City “Cultural Properties”) {{shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)|version=97899421|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March) == Access == * From the {{ill|Seibu Ikebukuro Line|en|Seibu Ikebukuro Line|WD=Q93546|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line}} at {{ill|Hōya Station|en|Hōya Station|WD=Q843153|lt=Hayamachi Station}} South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Bus|1=Midori Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279068}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[:en:Toei Oedo Line|Toei Oedo Line]] at {{ill|Hikarigaoka Station|en|Hikarigaoka Station|WD=Q1132210|lt=Hikarigaoka Station}}, take a bus for 19 minutes. == References ==
{{Reflist}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Oizumi Yasaka jinja]][[ja:八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Oizumi Yasaka jinja]][[ja:八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Oizumi Yasaka jinja]][[ja:八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q85876838]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | native_name = {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} | image = File:Yasaka Jinja Nerima.jpg | image description = Main Hall of Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}} {{ill|Nerima|en|Nerima|WD=Q232655|lt=Nerima Ward}} {{ill|Ōizumi, Gunma|en|Ōizumi, Gunma|WD=Q385080|lt=Oizumi-cho}} | address = 1-44-1, Oizumi-cho | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}} | temple_Ranking = (if any; here given as "Village Shrine") | founded = | subsidiary shrines = {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}}, {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|WD=Q3471785|lt=Asama Shrine}}, {{ill|Mikudate Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikudate Shrine|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} | alternate name = | festival = Main festival (example: Grand Festival around June 15) | coordinates = {{Coord|35.76722|N|139.60611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Nerima-ku|WD=Q232655}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine}}) in Oizumi-cho, Honma District is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine}} located in Oizumi-cho, Nerima Ward, Tokyo. It is a village shrine and is famed for legends concerning a curse for those who eat melons, due to its association with the “Gion” tradition. == Deity == The primary deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}. In addition, three subsidiary deities enshrined in the subsidiary shrines are: * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|稲蒼魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ウカノミタマ}} (at the Inari Shrine) * {{ill|Konohana Sakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohana Sakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (at the Asama Shrine) * {{ill|Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|ja|国常立命|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=国之常立神}} (at the Mikudate Shrine) == History == Its founding date is unknown. The {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵国風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=新編武蔵風土記稿|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} also records that “the history of the shrine’s establishment is unknown” (see also {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village, Tennōsha}}). In that text it is also referred to as “Tennōsha.” The name was changed to Yasaka Shrine in the early Meiji period due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}. == Grounds == An image of a “Fujizuka” (a mound resembling Mt. Fuji) known as “Nakazato Fuji” stands on the right-hand side of the shrine’s main hall. This mound was constructed by the local 【ill|Fuji-kō|ja|富士講】 group from the Edo period to early Showa period. A stone monument dated to 1822 ({{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei}} 5) within the mound suggests that its construction began in the Edo period and was later completed in the early Meiji period by the local {{ill|Marukichi-kō|ja|丸吉講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marukichi-kō|1=Marukichi-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368055}}. Local traditional events are still celebrated by the descendants of the Fuji-kō. Inside the ema hall, several votive plaques and pilgrimage pictures (ema) dating from the Edo period display realistic depictions of the shrine’s historical scenery. == Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around June 15 (example: “Reidai-sai”) * Other annual events include Bon Odori in July, and a “Yamate no Fuji” opening event in August – the largest mound in the district, which has been designated as a tangible folk cultural property. * The “Nakazato Hayashi,” a traditional festival inherited from the Edo period, is still performed during the New Year at the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Folk Cultural Property (Fukuoka Prefecture Designation) === * Nakazato Fuji (Fujizuka): One of the larger mounds built by the Fuji-kō (a group of pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. It stands 12 meters tall (from the base on its south side) with a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument from Bunsei 5 (1822) indicates it existed during the Edo period. Among the 36 stone structures, the monument of the Dōso-gami is unique in the district. === Treasures === * Ema (votive plaques) commemorating the subjugation of the eight‐headed serpent (dating from Tenpō 5, January) * Ema commemorating Mt. Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March) == Access == * From the {{ill|Seibu Ikebukuro Line|en|Seibu Ikebukuro Line|WD=Q93546|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line}} at {{ill|Hōya Station|en|Hōya Station|WD=Q843153|lt=Hayamachi Station}} South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Bus|1=Midori Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279068}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[:en:Toei Oedo Line|Toei Oedo Line]] at {{ill|Hikarigaoka Station|en|Hikarigaoka Station|WD=Q1132210|lt=Hikarigaoka Station}}, take a bus for 19 minutes. == References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Volume 134, Shinza District No.6|chapter=Hashido Village Tennōsha|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village Tennōsha}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} * {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.miyama.lg.jp/info/prev.asp?fol_id=2168 Ema and Great Lanterns] (Miyama City “Cultural Properties”) {{shinto-stub}}

It is a village shrine and is famed for legends concerning a curse for those who eat melons, due to its association with the “Gion” tradition. == Deity == The primary deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}. In addition, three subsidiary deities enshrined in the subsidiary shrines are: * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|稲蒼魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ウカノミタマ}} (at the Inari Shrine) * {{ill|Konohana Sakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohana Sakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (at the Asama Shrine) * {{ill|Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|ja|国常立命|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=国之常立神}} (at the Mikudate Shrine) == History == Its founding date is unknown. The {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵国風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=新編武蔵風土記稿|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} also records that “the history of the shrine’s establishment is unknown” (see also {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village, Tennōsha}}). In that text it is also referred to as “Tennōsha.” The name was changed to Yasaka Shrine in the early Meiji period due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}. == Grounds == An image of a “Fujizuka” (a mound resembling Mt. Fuji) known as “Nakazato Fuji” stands on the right-hand side of the shrine’s main hall. This mound was constructed by the local 【ill|Fuji-kō|ja|富士講】 group from the Edo period to early Showa period. A stone monument dated to 1822 ({{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei}} 5) within the mound suggests that its construction began in the Edo period and was later completed in the early Meiji period by the local {{ill|Marukichi-kō|ja|丸吉講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marukichi-kō|1=Marukichi-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368055}}. Local traditional events are still celebrated by the descendants of the Fuji-kō. Inside the ema hall, several votive plaques and pilgrimage pictures (ema) dating from the Edo period display realistic depictions of the shrine’s historical scenery. == Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around June 15 (example: “Reidai-sai”) * Other annual events include Bon Odori in July, and a “Yamate no Fuji” opening event in August – the largest mound in the district, which has been designated as a tangible folk cultural property. * The “Nakazato Hayashi,” a traditional festival inherited from the Edo period, is still performed during the New Year at the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Folk Cultural Property (Fukuoka Prefecture Designation) === * Nakazato Fuji (Fujizuka): One of the larger mounds built by the Fuji-kō (a group of pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. It stands 12 meters tall (from the base on its south side) with a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument from Bunsei 5 (1822) indicates it existed during the Edo period. Among the 36 stone structures, the monument of the Dōso-gami is unique in the district. === Treasures === * Ema (votive plaques) commemorating the subjugation of the eight‐headed serpent (dating from Tenpō 5, January) * Ema commemorating Mt. Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March) == Access == * From the {{ill|Seibu Ikebukuro Line|en|Seibu Ikebukuro Line|WD=Q93546|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line}} at {{ill|Hōya Station|en|Hōya Station|WD=Q843153|lt=Hayamachi Station}} South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Bus|1=Midori Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279068}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[:en:Toei Oedo Line|Toei Oedo Line]] at {{ill|Hikarigaoka Station|en|Hikarigaoka Station|WD=Q1132210|lt=Hikarigaoka Station}}, take a bus for 19 minutes. == References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Volume 134, Shinza District No.6|chapter=Hashido Village Tennōsha|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village Tennōsha}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} * {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.miyama.lg.jp/info/prev.asp?fol_id=2168 Ema and Great Lanterns] (Miyama City “Cultural Properties”) {{shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)|version=97899421|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | native_name = {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} | image = File:Yasaka Jinja Nerima.jpg | image description = Main Hall of Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}} {{ill|Nerima|en|Nerima|WD=Q232655|lt=Nerima Ward}} {{ill|Ōizumi, Gunma|en|Ōizumi, Gunma|WD=Q385080|lt=Oizumi-cho}} | address = 1-44-1, Oizumi-cho | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}} | temple_Ranking = (if any; here given as "Village Shrine") | founded = | subsidiary shrines = {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}}, {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|WD=Q3471785|lt=Asama Shrine}}, {{ill|Mikudate Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikudate Shrine|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} | alternate name = | festival = Main festival (example: Grand Festival around June 15) | coordinates = {{Coord|35.76722|N|139.60611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Nerima-ku|WD=Q232655}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine}}) in Oizumi-cho, Honma District is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine}} located in Oizumi-cho, Nerima Ward, Tokyo. It is a village shrine and is famed for legends concerning a curse for those who eat melons, due to its association with the “Gion” tradition. == Deity == The primary deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}. In addition, three subsidiary deities enshrined in the subsidiary shrines are: * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|稲蒼魂命|1=Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ウカノミタマ}} (at the Inari Shrine) * {{ill|Konohana Sakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohana Sakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (at the Asama Shrine) * {{ill|Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|ja|国常立命|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=国之常立神}} (at the Mikudate Shrine) == History == Its founding date is unknown. The {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵国風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=新編武蔵風土記稿|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} also records that “the history of the shrine’s establishment is unknown” (see also {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village, Tennōsha}}). In that text it is also referred to as “Tennōsha.” The name was changed to Yasaka Shrine in the early Meiji period due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}. == Grounds == An image of a “Fujizuka” (a mound resembling Mt. Fuji) known as “Nakazato Fuji” stands on the right-hand side of the shrine’s main hall. This mound was constructed by the local 【ill|Fuji-kō|ja|富士講】 group from the Edo period to early Showa period. A stone monument dated to 1822 ({{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei}} 5) within the mound suggests that its construction began in the Edo period and was later completed in the early Meiji period by the local {{ill|Marukichi-kō|ja|丸吉講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marukichi-kō|1=Marukichi-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368055}}. Local traditional events are still celebrated by the descendants of the Fuji-kō. Inside the ema hall, several votive plaques and pilgrimage pictures (ema) dating from the Edo period display realistic depictions of the shrine’s historical scenery. == Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around June 15 (example: “Reidai-sai”) * Other annual events include Bon Odori in July, and a “Yamate no Fuji” opening event in August – the largest mound in the district, which has been designated as a tangible folk cultural property. * The “Nakazato Hayashi,” a traditional festival inherited from the Edo period, is still performed during the New Year at the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Folk Cultural Property (Fukuoka Prefecture Designation) === * Nakazato Fuji (Fujizuka): One of the larger mounds built by the Fuji-kō (a group of pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. It stands 12 meters tall (from the base on its south side) with a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument from Bunsei 5 (1822) indicates it existed during the Edo period. Among the 36 stone structures, the monument of the Dōso-gami is unique in the district. === Treasures === * Ema (votive plaques) commemorating the subjugation of the eight‐headed serpent (dating from Tenpō 5, January) * Ema commemorating Mt. Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March) == Access == * From the {{ill|Seibu Ikebukuro Line|en|Seibu Ikebukuro Line|WD=Q93546|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line}} at {{ill|Hōya Station|en|Hōya Station|WD=Q843153|lt=Hayamachi Station}} South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Bus|1=Midori Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279068}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3‑minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[:en:Toei Oedo Line|Toei Oedo Line]] at {{ill|Hikarigaoka Station|en|Hikarigaoka Station|WD=Q1132210|lt=Hikarigaoka Station}}, take a bus for 19 minutes. == References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Volume 134, Shinza District No.6|chapter=Hashido Village Tennōsha|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village Tennōsha}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} * {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.miyama.lg.jp/info/prev.asp?fol_id=2168 Ema and Great Lanterns] (Miyama City “Cultural Properties”) {{shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)|version=97899421|comment=Imported full ja history}}
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Oizumi Yasaka jinja}} [[ja:八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)]]

Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March) == Access == * From the {{ill|Seibu Ikebukuro Line|en|Seibu Ikebukuro Line|WD=Q93546|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line|1=Seibu Ikebukuro Line}} at {{ill|Hōya Station|en|Hōya Station|WD=Q843153|lt=Hayamachi Station|1=Hōya Station}} South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Bus|1=Midori Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279068}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3-minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[:en:Toei Oedo Line|Toei Oedo Line]] at {{ill|Hikarigaoka Station|en|Hikarigaoka Station|WD=Q1132210|lt=Hikarigaoka Station|1=Hikarigaoka Station}}, take a bus for 19 minutes. == References ==
{{Reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Oizumi Yasaka jinja]][[ja:八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Oizumi Yasaka jinja]][[ja:八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Oizumi Yasaka jinja]][[ja:八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)]]
{{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | native_name = {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} | image = File:Yasaka Jinja Nerima.jpg | image description = Main Hall of Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}} {{ill|Nerima|en|Nerima|WD=Q232655|lt=Nerima Ward|1=Nerima}} {{ill|Ōizumi, Gunma|en|Ōizumi, Gunma|WD=Q385080|lt=Oizumi-cho|1=Ōizumi, Gunma}} | address = 1-44-1, Oizumi-cho | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}} | temple_Ranking = (if any; here given as "Village Shrine") | founded = | subsidiary shrines = {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}}, {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|WD=Q3471785|lt=Asama Shrine|1=Sengen shrine}}, {{ill|Mikudate Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikudate Shrine|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} | alternate name = | festival = Main festival (example: Grand Festival around June 15) | coordinates = {{Coord|35.76722|N|139.60611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Nerima-ku|WD=Q232655|1=Nerima}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}}) in Oizumi-cho, Honma District is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Oizumi-cho, Nerima Ward, Tokyo. It is a village shrine and is famed for legends concerning a curse for those who eat melons, due to its association with the “Gion” tradition. == Deity == The primary deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}. In addition, three subsidiary deities enshrined in the subsidiary shrines are: * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|稲蒼魂命|1=Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ウカノミタマ}} (at the Inari Shrine) * {{ill|Konohana Sakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohana Sakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (at the Asama Shrine) * {{ill|Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|ja|国常立命|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=国之常立神}} (at the Mikudate Shrine) == History == Its founding date is unknown. The {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵国風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=新編武蔵風土記稿|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} also records that “the history of the shrine’s establishment is unknown” (see also {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village, Tennōsha}}). In that text it is also referred to as “Tennōsha.” The name was changed to Yasaka Shrine in the early Meiji period due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=神仏分離|1=Shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}. == Grounds == An image of a “Fujizuka” (a mound resembling Mt. Fuji) known as “Nakazato Fuji” stands on the right-hand side of the shrine’s main hall. This mound was constructed by the local 【ill|Fuji-kō|ja|富士講】 group from the Edo period to early Showa period. A stone monument dated to 1822 ({{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei|1=Bunsei}} 5) within the mound suggests that its construction began in the Edo period and was later completed in the early Meiji period by the local {{ill|Marukichi-kō|ja|丸吉講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marukichi-kō|1=Q11368055|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368055}}. Local traditional events are still celebrated by the descendants of the Fuji-kō. Inside the ema hall, several votive plaques and pilgrimage pictures (ema) dating from the Edo period display realistic depictions of the shrine’s historical scenery. == Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around June 15 (example: “Reidai-sai”) * Other annual events include Bon Odori in July, and a “Yamate no Fuji” opening event in August – the largest mound in the district, which has been designated as a tangible folk cultural property. * The “Nakazato Hayashi,” a traditional festival inherited from the Edo period, is still performed during the New Year at the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Folk Cultural Property (Fukuoka Prefecture Designation) === * Nakazato Fuji (Fujizuka): One of the larger mounds built by the Fuji-kō (a group of pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. It stands 12 meters tall (from the base on its south side) with a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument from Bunsei 5 (1822) indicates it existed during the Edo period. Among the 36 stone structures, the monument of the Dōso-gami is unique in the district. === Treasures === * Ema (votive plaques) commemorating the subjugation of the eight‐headed serpent (dating from Tenpō 5, January) * Ema commemorating Mt. Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March) == Access == * From the {{ill|Seibu Ikebukuro Line|en|Seibu Ikebukuro Line|WD=Q93546|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line|1=Seibu Ikebukuro Line}} at {{ill|Hōya Station|en|Hōya Station|WD=Q843153|lt=Hayamachi Station|1=Hōya Station}} South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Bus|1=Midori Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279068}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3-minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[:en:Toei Oedo Line|Toei Oedo Line]] at {{ill|Hikarigaoka Station|en|Hikarigaoka Station|WD=Q1132210|lt=Hikarigaoka Station|1=Hikarigaoka Station}}, take a bus for 19 minutes. == References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Volume 134, Shinza District No.6|chapter=Hashido Village Tennōsha|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village Tennōsha}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}} * {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith|1=Gion Faith}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.miyama.lg.jp/info/prev.asp?fol_id=2168 Ema and Great Lanterns] (Miyama City “Cultural Properties”) {{shinto-stub}}

{{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | native_name = {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} | image = File:Yasaka Jinja Nerima.jpg | image description = Main Hall of Yasaka Shrine (Oizumi-cho, Honma District) | location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}} {{ill|Nerima|en|Nerima|WD=Q232655|lt=Nerima Ward|1=Nerima}} {{ill|Ōizumi, Gunma|en|Ōizumi, Gunma|WD=Q385080|lt=Oizumi-cho|1=Ōizumi, Gunma}} | address = 1-44-1, Oizumi-cho | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}} | temple_Ranking = (if any; here given as "Village Shrine") | founded = | subsidiary shrines = {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}}, {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|WD=Q3471785|lt=Asama Shrine|1=Sengen shrine}}, {{ill|Mikudate Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikudate Shrine|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} | alternate name = | festival = Main festival (example: Grand Festival around June 15) | coordinates = {{Coord|35.76722|N|139.60611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Nerima-ku|WD=Q232655|1=Nerima}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}}) in Oizumi-cho, Honma District is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Oizumi-cho, Nerima Ward, Tokyo. It is a village shrine and is famed for legends concerning a curse for those who eat melons, due to its association with the “Gion” tradition. == Deity == The primary deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}. In addition, three subsidiary deities enshrined in the subsidiary shrines are: * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|稲蒼魂命|1=Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ウカノミタマ}} (at the Inari Shrine) * {{ill|Konohana Sakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohana Sakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (at the Asama Shrine) * {{ill|Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|ja|国常立命|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukuri-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=国之常立神}} (at the Mikudate Shrine) == History == Its founding date is unknown. The {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵国風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=新編武蔵風土記稿|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} also records that “the history of the shrine’s establishment is unknown” (see also {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village, Tennōsha}}). In that text it is also referred to as “Tennōsha.” The name was changed to Yasaka Shrine in the early Meiji period due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=神仏分離|1=Shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}. == Grounds == An image of a “Fujizuka” (a mound resembling Mt. Fuji) known as “Nakazato Fuji” stands on the right-hand side of the shrine’s main hall. This mound was constructed by the local 【ill|Fuji-kō|ja|富士講】 group from the Edo period to early Showa period. A stone monument dated to 1822 ({{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei|1=Bunsei}} 5) within the mound suggests that its construction began in the Edo period and was later completed in the early Meiji period by the local {{ill|Marukichi-kō|ja|丸吉講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marukichi-kō|1=Q11368055|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368055}}. Local traditional events are still celebrated by the descendants of the Fuji-kō. Inside the ema hall, several votive plaques and pilgrimage pictures (ema) dating from the Edo period display realistic depictions of the shrine’s historical scenery. == Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around June 15 (example: “Reidai-sai”) * Other annual events include Bon Odori in July, and a “Yamate no Fuji” opening event in August – the largest mound in the district, which has been designated as a tangible folk cultural property. * The “Nakazato Hayashi,” a traditional festival inherited from the Edo period, is still performed during the New Year at the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Folk Cultural Property (Fukuoka Prefecture Designation) === * Nakazato Fuji (Fujizuka): One of the larger mounds built by the Fuji-kō (a group of pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. It stands 12 meters tall (from the base on its south side) with a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument from Bunsei 5 (1822) indicates it existed during the Edo period. Among the 36 stone structures, the monument of the Dōso-gami is unique in the district. === Treasures === * Ema (votive plaques) commemorating the subjugation of the eight‐headed serpent (dating from Tenpō 5, January) * Ema commemorating Mt. Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March) == Access == * From the {{ill|Seibu Ikebukuro Line|en|Seibu Ikebukuro Line|WD=Q93546|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line|1=Seibu Ikebukuro Line}} at {{ill|Hōya Station|en|Hōya Station|WD=Q843153|lt=Hayamachi Station|1=Hōya Station}} South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Bus|1=Midori Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279068}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3-minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[:en:Toei Oedo Line|Toei Oedo Line]] at {{ill|Hikarigaoka Station|en|Hikarigaoka Station|WD=Q1132210|lt=Hikarigaoka Station|1=Hikarigaoka Station}}, take a bus for 19 minutes. == References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Volume 134, Shinza District No.6|chapter=Hashido Village Tennōsha|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|id={{NDLJP|763996/78}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō, Hashido Village Tennōsha}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}} * {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith|1=Gion Faith}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.miyama.lg.jp/info/prev.asp?fol_id=2168 Ema and Great Lanterns] (Miyama City “Cultural Properties”) {{shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (練馬区大泉町)|version=97899421|comment=Imported full ja history}}

A stone monument from Bunsei 5 (1822) indicates it existed during the Edo period. Among the 36 stone structures, the monument of the Dōso-gami is unique in the district. === Treasures === * Ema (votive plaques) commemorating the subjugation of the eight‐headed serpent (dating from Tenpō 5, January) * Ema commemorating Mt. Fuji ascents (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting the pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March) == Access == * From the {{ill|Seibu Ikebukuro Line|en|Seibu Ikebukuro Line|WD=Q93546|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line|1=Seibu Ikebukuro Line}} at {{ill|Hōya Station|en|Hōya Station|WD=Q843153|lt=Hayamachi Station|1=Hōya Station}} South Exit, take the {{ill|Midori Bus|ja|みどりバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Bus|1=Midori Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279068}} to “Bessobashi” (a 3-minute walk from the bus stop)＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/rekishiwoshiru/rekishibunkazai/kakarikara/prnerima/dai30.pdf|title=Nerima Cultural Property No.30|publisher=City of Nerima|accessdate=2019-11-02|format=PDF}}＜/ref＞ * Or, from the [[:en:Toei Oedo Line|Toei Oedo Line]] at {{ill|Hikarigaoka Station|en|Hikarigaoka Station|WD=Q1132210|lt=Hikarigaoka Station|1=Hikarigaoka Station}}, take a bus for 19 minutes. == References ==
